meaning: n.(用鞭)快速的轻打,轻打声,弹开v.轻弹,轻轻拂去,忽然摇动,(翅)拍动,(旗)飘扬
examples: Snuggle as you watch a romantic flick together.
He threw the ball back with a quick flick of the wrist.
I had a flick through the catalogue while I was waiting.
Guille Franco sent a flick against an upright on the hour as Villarreal provided stubborn resistance.
In addition to disappointing box office turnout, the Redford-directed flick earned largely negative reviews.
